metadata.source: Coxhead, A. (2000). A new academic word list. TESOL Quarterly, 34(2), 213-238.
metadata.enrichment_source: Măchiță, O.-M. (2021). The Acquisition of English Phonology by Romanian and French Learners of English. University of Bucharest.
metadata.description: Academic Word List — Enriched Format. Pronunciation, synonyms, definitions, difficulty levels, categories, and generic_replacements calibrated on Romanian-English phonological interference (Măchiță, 2021).

vocabulary.definition: To examine something carefully and in detail to understand it
vocabulary.example_sentence: We need to analyse the data carefully before drawing conclusions.

vocabulary.definition: A way of dealing with something; a method or strategy
vocabulary.example_sentence: We need a different approach to this problem.

vocabulary.definition: A particular field, subject, or section of activity
vocabulary.example_sentence: This is a complex area of research.

vocabulary.definition: To evaluate or judge the quality, value, or importance of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: The teacher will assess your progress this week.

vocabulary.definition: To suppose or believe something to be true without proof
vocabulary.example_sentence: Don't assume you know the answer without checking.

vocabulary.definition: The power or right to give orders and make decisions
vocabulary.example_sentence: She has the authority to approve the project budget.

vocabulary.definition: Able to be used, obtained, or accessed; ready for use
vocabulary.example_sentence: Is this resource available online for students?

vocabulary.definition: An advantage, profit, or improvement gained from something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Exercise has many health benefits for adults.

vocabulary.definition: An abstract idea or general principle used to understand something

vocabulary.definition: To be composed or made up of particular elements or parts
vocabulary.example_sentence: The team consists of five experienced researchers.

vocabulary.definition: To be or form the parts that make up something

vocabulary.definition: The circumstances or background that help explain an event or action

vocabulary.definition: A formal legally binding agreement between two or more parties

vocabulary.definition: To bring something into existence; to make or produce something new

vocabulary.definition: Facts, statistics, and information used for analysis or reference
vocabulary.example_sentence: The data shows a clear trend towards improvement.

vocabulary.definition: To state the exact meaning of a word, concept, or term

vocabulary.definition: To obtain or receive something from a source; to originate from

vocabulary.definition: To give out or spread something among a number of recipients

vocabulary.definition: The system of production, trade, and consumption in a country

vocabulary.definition: The surroundings and conditions in which a person or thing exists
vocabulary.example_sentence: We must protect the environment for future generations.

vocabulary.definition: To set up or create something that is intended to last permanently
vocabulary.example_sentence: They established a new research institute last year.

vocabulary.definition: To make an approximate calculation or rough assessment of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: I estimate it will take approximately two hours to complete.

vocabulary.definition: Clearly seen or understood; obvious and apparent

vocabulary.definition: To send goods, services, or products to another country for sale
vocabulary.example_sentence: The country exports a large volume of agricultural goods.

vocabulary.definition: An element, aspect, or circumstance that contributes to a result

vocabulary.definition: To provide money for a project or activity; money management
vocabulary.example_sentence: We need additional resources to finance this research project.

vocabulary.definition: A set method, rule, or equation used to produce a result
vocabulary.example_sentence: There is no simple formula for achieving fluency in a language.

vocabulary.definition: The purpose, role, or intended use of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: What is the primary function of this button in the interface?

vocabulary.definition: To recognize, name, or determine who or what something is
vocabulary.example_sentence: Can you identify the main cause of the pronunciation error?

vocabulary.definition: Money received regularly as payment for work or investments

vocabulary.definition: To show, point out, or suggest something clearly
vocabulary.example_sentence: The research results indicate a significant improvement.

vocabulary.definition: A single person considered separately from a group

vocabulary.definition: To explain or understand the meaning of something

vocabulary.definition: To include something as a necessary part or requirement

vocabulary.definition: An important topic or problem that needs consideration

vocabulary.definition: Physical or mental work and effort; the workforce

vocabulary.definition: Permitted by law; relating to the law

vocabulary.definition: To make or enact laws through a formal process
vocabulary.example_sentence: The government will legislate on data privacy this year.

vocabulary.definition: More important, serious, or significant than others

vocabulary.definition: A systematic way or procedure for doing something

vocabulary.definition: To happen or take place at a particular time

vocabulary.definition: A course of action adopted by an organization or government

vocabulary.definition: A fundamental truth or rule that guides behavior or reasoning
vocabulary.example_sentence: This is the core principle underlying all language acquisition.

vocabulary.definition: To continue or move forward with an action or plan
vocabulary.example_sentence: Please proceed with caution when attempting this exercise.

vocabulary.definition: A series of actions or steps taken in order to achieve a result
vocabulary.example_sentence: Language acquisition is a complex and gradual process.

vocabulary.definition: To need something as a necessity or condition

vocabulary.definition: Careful investigation to discover new facts or test ideas
vocabulary.example_sentence: Recent research demonstrates that shadowing improves fluency.

vocabulary.definition: To reply or react to something said or done
vocabulary.example_sentence: How did you respond to the critical feedback?

vocabulary.definition: The function, part, or position played by someone or something

vocabulary.definition: A distinct part or subdivision of something larger

vocabulary.definition: A distinct area or division of an economy or society
vocabulary.example_sentence: She works in the public education sector.

vocabulary.definition: Important, meaningful, or large enough to deserve attention
vocabulary.example_sentence: There was a significant improvement in pronunciation scores.

vocabulary.definition: Having a resemblance or likeness but not being identical
vocabulary.example_sentence: Romanian and English have similar stress patterns in some words.

vocabulary.definition: The origin, cause, or place from which something comes

vocabulary.definition: Clearly defined, identified, or limited; precise and exact

vocabulary.definition: The way something is organized, arranged, or put together

vocabulary.definition: A system of ideas intended to explain something based on evidence

vocabulary.definition: To differ, change, or be different from one another

vocabulary.definition: To successfully accomplish a goal through effort and determination

vocabulary.definition: To obtain, develop, or come to have knowledge or a skill

vocabulary.definition: To manage or organize the operations of an organization

vocabulary.definition: To influence or have an impact on something or someone

vocabulary.definition: Suitable or fitting for a particular purpose or situation

vocabulary.definition: A particular element, feature, or side of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Consider every aspect of the language learning plan.

vocabulary.definition: To help or give support to someone doing something

vocabulary.definition: A class or group of things sharing common characteristics

vocabulary.definition: A main section or division of a book or document
vocabulary.example_sentence: Read chapter three of the phonetics textbook before class.

vocabulary.definition: An official task or assignment given to a person or group
vocabulary.example_sentence: She was given a commission to review the curriculum.

vocabulary.definition: A group of people sharing a common location, interest, or identity
vocabulary.example_sentence: The learning community provides mutual support and encouragement.

vocabulary.definition: Consisting of many interconnected parts; not simple to understand
vocabulary.example_sentence: English phonology can be remarkably complex for Romanian speakers.

vocabulary.definition: To calculate or determine something using a mathematical method
vocabulary.example_sentence: The system will compute your accuracy score automatically.

vocabulary.definition: To reach a decision or judgment; to bring something to an end
vocabulary.example_sentence: We can conclude from the data that shadowing is effective.

vocabulary.definition: To organize or carry out an activity in a systematic way
vocabulary.example_sentence: She conducted the pronunciation experiment with great precision.

vocabulary.definition: Following as a direct result or effect of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: The consequent improvement in her accent was noticeable.

vocabulary.definition: To build or put together something from separate components
vocabulary.example_sentence: Construct a coherent argument to support your position.

vocabulary.definition: To use up a resource completely; to eat or drink
vocabulary.example_sentence: Intensive language learning consumes considerable time and energy.

vocabulary.definition: Acknowledgement given for achieving something; recognition
vocabulary.example_sentence: Give academic credit to all sources you reference.

vocabulary.definition: The customs, arts, and social institutions of a particular group
vocabulary.example_sentence: Language and culture are deeply and inseparably linked.

vocabulary.definition: To plan and create something with a specific purpose in mind
vocabulary.example_sentence: She designed a user-friendly interface for the learning app.

vocabulary.definition: Clearly different, separate, or recognizable from others
vocabulary.example_sentence: These are two entirely distinct phonological categories.

vocabulary.definition: A basic or essential part of something larger
vocabulary.example_sentence: Vocabulary is a key element of communicative fluency.

vocabulary.definition: To consider or treat two things as equivalent or the same
vocabulary.example_sentence: Don't equate speaking speed with communicative competence.

vocabulary.definition: To assess or determine the value, quality, or significance of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Evaluate your own pronunciation performance critically.

vocabulary.definition: A distinctive quality, characteristic, or aspect of something

vocabulary.definition: Coming at the end; last in a series
vocabulary.example_sentence: This is the final stage of the pronunciation assessment.

vocabulary.definition: To concentrate attention or effort on something specific
vocabulary.example_sentence: Focus specifically on your vowel pronunciation today.

vocabulary.definition: A strong or significant effect or influence on something

vocabulary.definition: To cause physical harm, damage, or hurt to someone
vocabulary.example_sentence: Poor vocal technique can injure the larynx over time.

vocabulary.definition: An organization established for a particular purpose or field
vocabulary.example_sentence: She studies linguistics at a prestigious language institute.

vocabulary.definition: To dedicate time, money, or effort to achieve a future benefit

vocabulary.definition: An individual article, object, or unit in a collection
vocabulary.example_sentence: Review each vocabulary item on the list systematically.

vocabulary.definition: A regular written record; an academic publication or periodical
vocabulary.example_sentence: Keep a vocabulary journal of all new words you encounter.

vocabulary.definition: To keep something in its current state; to preserve or sustain
vocabulary.example_sentence: Maintain a consistently positive attitude towards your mistakes.

vocabulary.definition: Conforming to a usual or standard pattern; typical

vocabulary.definition: To get, acquire, or come to possess something
vocabulary.example_sentence: You can obtain additional resources from the library online.

vocabulary.definition: To take part or become actively involved in an activity
vocabulary.example_sentence: Everyone should participate actively in the speaking exercises.

vocabulary.definition: To become aware of or notice something through the senses
vocabulary.example_sentence: Learners perceive /θ/ as similar to /t/ due to L1 transfer.

vocabulary.definition: Constructive, affirmative, or having a good and beneficial effect

vocabulary.definition: Having the capacity or possibility to develop into something

vocabulary.definition: Coming or happening before in time or order

vocabulary.definition: Most important, fundamental, or first in order

vocabulary.definition: To buy something by paying money for it
vocabulary.example_sentence: She purchased an advanced phonetics reference book.

vocabulary.definition: The scope, extent, or variety of something

vocabulary.definition: A specific geographic area or territory
vocabulary.example_sentence: Accents vary considerably depending on the speaker's region.

vocabulary.definition: To control or supervise something according to rules
vocabulary.example_sentence: Regulate your speaking speed to ensure clarity.

vocabulary.definition: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand
vocabulary.example_sentence: Provide relevant and specific examples to support your argument.

vocabulary.definition: To live in or be located at a particular place permanently
vocabulary.example_sentence: She resides in an English-speaking country now.

vocabulary.definition: A stock of materials, information, or assets that can be used
vocabulary.example_sentence: Utilize all available learning resources to maximize progress.

vocabulary.definition: To limit or control the size, amount, or range of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Don't restrict yourself to just one learning method.

vocabulary.definition: Free from danger; firmly fixed; to make safe
vocabulary.example_sentence: Feel secure and confident when speaking English publicly.

vocabulary.definition: To try to find or obtain something through effort
vocabulary.example_sentence: Actively seek constructive feedback from native speakers.

vocabulary.definition: To carefully choose from a number of options
vocabulary.example_sentence: Select the most appropriate vocabulary for the context.

vocabulary.definition: A location where something exists or occurs

vocabulary.definition: A plan designed to achieve a long-term goal

vocabulary.definition: An investigation into the opinions or experiences of a group

vocabulary.definition: Written or printed words; a written document or book

vocabulary.definition: A long-established custom or practice passed through generations

vocabulary.definition: To move or apply something from one context to another

vocabulary.definition: One of two or more available options or choices

vocabulary.definition: A fact or condition connected with an event or situation

vocabulary.definition: A verbal or written remark expressing an opinion

vocabulary.definition: To make up for a deficiency or loss with something equal

vocabulary.definition: A part or element that makes up a larger whole

vocabulary.definition: Permission or agreement given voluntarily for something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Obtain informed consent before recording participants.

vocabulary.definition: Large in size, amount, or extent; significant
vocabulary.example_sentence: She made considerable academic progress this semester.

vocabulary.definition: Occurring continuously without interruption or change

vocabulary.definition: To restrict, limit, or severely restrict the freedom of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Fear can constrain your ability to speak spontaneously.

vocabulary.definition: To give or add something to a shared effort or collection

vocabulary.definition: To come together or assemble formally for a meeting

vocabulary.definition: To organize activities so they work together efficiently
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coordinate your breathing pattern with your speech rhythm.

vocabulary.definition: The central, most important, or fundamental part of something

vocabulary.definition: Relating to a large company or business organization
vocabulary.example_sentence: Use formal corporate language in professional settings.

vocabulary.definition: To match, agree with, or be similar to something else

vocabulary.definition: The standards or principles used to judge or evaluate something
vocabulary.example_sentence: What criteria are used to assess speaking proficiency?

vocabulary.definition: To reach a logical conclusion based on available evidence
vocabulary.example_sentence: You can deduce the meaning of unfamiliar words from context.

vocabulary.definition: To show or prove something clearly through action or evidence
vocabulary.example_sentence: Demonstrate your comprehension through spoken responses.

vocabulary.definition: To record something in writing for future reference

vocabulary.definition: To have or exert a commanding control or influence over something

vocabulary.definition: Special importance or prominence given to something

vocabulary.definition: To make certain that something will happen or be the case

vocabulary.definition: To deliberately leave out or not include something

vocabulary.definition: A basic structure or system of ideas used as a foundation

vocabulary.definition: A sum of money set aside for a purpose; to provide money for

vocabulary.definition: To make something clear by giving examples or visual explanation
vocabulary.example_sentence: Illustrate your point with a concrete and specific example.

vocabulary.definition: To come to live permanently in a foreign country

vocabulary.definition: To suggest something indirectly rather than stating it explicitly

vocabulary.definition: A particular example or single occurrence of something

vocabulary.definition: To communicate or work with others; to have a mutual effect

vocabulary.definition: To show or prove that something is reasonable or necessary

vocabulary.definition: A sheet, level, or thickness of something lying over another

vocabulary.definition: A connection or relationship between two things

vocabulary.definition: To find the exact position of something

vocabulary.definition: To increase something to the greatest possible level
vocabulary.example_sentence: Maximise every available opportunity to practise speaking.

vocabulary.definition: Lesser in importance, seriousness, or size

vocabulary.definition: To make something ineffective or invalid; to deny

vocabulary.definition: A result, consequence, or final product of an action

vocabulary.definition: A person who shares or participates in an activity with another

vocabulary.definition: A set of fundamental beliefs or guiding principles

vocabulary.definition: Relating to the body or material things rather than the mind
vocabulary.example_sentence: Speaking is fundamentally a physical and muscular skill.

vocabulary.definition: A part or share of a whole in relation to the total
vocabulary.example_sentence: A substantial proportion of pronunciation errors are phonemic.

vocabulary.definition: To prepare and issue printed or digital material for distribution

vocabulary.definition: To respond to something with a particular feeling or action

vocabulary.definition: A style of language used in a particular social context

vocabulary.definition: To depend on or trust something or someone for support

vocabulary.definition: To take away or eliminate something from a place or situation

vocabulary.definition: A systematic plan or arrangement for achieving something

vocabulary.definition: A particular order in which things follow one after another
vocabulary.example_sentence: Practise phonemes in a logical and deliberate sequence.

vocabulary.definition: The biological classification of organisms as male or female

vocabulary.definition: To move or change in position, direction, or focus

vocabulary.definition: To identify or describe precisely and in detail

vocabulary.definition: Enough in quantity or quality to meet a need

vocabulary.definition: A piece of work to be done or undertaken

vocabulary.definition: Relating to a specific skill, art, or practical subject
vocabulary.example_sentence: Use precise technical vocabulary with caution in conversation.

vocabulary.definition: A specific method or skill used to accomplish something

vocabulary.definition: The application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes
vocabulary.example_sentence: Modern technology significantly supports personalized language learning.

vocabulary.definition: Well-grounded, logical, and justifiable; legally acceptable

vocabulary.definition: The amount of something; the level of sound

vocabulary.definition: The ability or right to use, approach, or enter something

vocabulary.definition: Satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity for a purpose

vocabulary.definition: Occurring or done once every year
vocabulary.example_sentence: Complete a thorough annual review of your learning progress.

vocabulary.definition: Clearly visible, obvious, or easily understood

vocabulary.definition: Close to the actual but not exactly correct; nearly accurate

vocabulary.definition: A settled way of thinking or feeling about something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Your attitude towards making mistakes matters enormously.

vocabulary.definition: To regard something as being caused by a particular factor

vocabulary.definition: Relating to citizens; polite and courteous in manner
vocabulary.example_sentence: Be consistently civil and respectful in your conversations.

vocabulary.definition: A system of signals or rules for communication

vocabulary.definition: To dedicate oneself firmly to a course of action

vocabulary.definition: To convey or exchange information and ideas with others

vocabulary.definition: A series of events that repeat regularly in the same order

vocabulary.definition: Without being affected by; in spite of

vocabulary.definition: A measurable aspect or feature of a situation

vocabulary.definition: Relating to the home or family; within one's own country

vocabulary.definition: To come into existence, notice, or prominence

vocabulary.definition: A mistake or inaccuracy in speech, writing, or action

vocabulary.definition: Relating to a group sharing cultural, national, or racial identity

vocabulary.definition: An aim or desired result that you work towards

vocabulary.definition: To give or allow something formally; a sum of money given

vocabulary.definition: As a result or consequence; for this reason

vocabulary.definition: A proposed explanation or prediction made before investigation

vocabulary.definition: To put a plan, decision, or system into effect

vocabulary.definition: To show or suggest involvement in something

vocabulary.definition: To force something unwanted on someone; to place a burden

vocabulary.definition: To combine parts into a whole; to include as part of something

vocabulary.definition: Situated inside; relating to the inside of something
vocabulary.example_sentence: Internal motivation consistently drives superior learning results.
